intro: Urophyllium niveum, also known as White Urophyllum, is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits of anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Urophyllum niveum include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Urophyllum niveum is often used in tea, tincture, or capsule form, and is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion to leverage its medicinal properties.
